title: Operators and precedence deterministic evidence bank

items.title: Operators and precedence: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? Higher precedence means an operand is always evaluated earlier in time than every lower-precedence operand.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use precedence to determine grouping, not general evaluation order; rely only on documented sequencing and avoid multiple unsequenced accesses with side effects.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Use precedence to determine grouping, not general evaluation order; rely only on documented sequencing and avoid multiple unsequenced accesses with side effects.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Use precedence to determine grouping, not general evaluation order; rely only on documented sequencing and avoid multiple unsequenced accesses with side effects.
Higher precedence means an operand is always evaluated earlier in time than every lower-precedence operand.

answer.explanation: Use precedence to determine grouping, not general evaluation order; rely only on documented sequencing and avoid multiple unsequenced accesses with side effects.

items.title: Operators and precedence: executable application
items.prompt: Read one integer x, compute x multiplied by two and then increased by one using a clear grouped C expression, and print only the resulting integer.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Start from input validation and the declared observable output, then apply only the target language rule before considering optimization.

feedback.correct: All visible and hidden deterministic behavior checks passed for the bounded source task.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic behavior check failed; inspect bounds, input state, and the target rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every normal and boundary behavior test.
Applies Operators and precedence without undefined behavior or an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises existing bounded executable application retained from the authored language tranche under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
